mnemonics: SHARE until you CARE (Copy-on-write is used to SHARE resources until a process CAREs enough to write and needs its own copy)
keyPoints: A resource management technique that delays copying data until a write operation is performed.
Initially, multiple processes share a single copy of a resource (e.g., a memory page) for reading.
Only when a process attempts to write to the shared resource is a private copy created for that process.
commonMistakes: Assuming a copy is made immediately upon a fork() call, rather than only on a subsequent write.
Confusing it with simple memory sharing; it is a specific optimization that defers the copy cost.
Believing the original and copied resources remain linked after the write; they become independent.
